changes.before: "It isn't one bad chain," Jaina said.
changes.after: "It's spread wider than that," Jaina said.

changes.before: "We're not ahead of this," Sylvanas said. "We're reading a road after the wagons passed."
changes.after: "We're reading a road after the wagons passed," Sylvanas said.

changes.before: It wasn't. No carts. No smoke. No voices.
changes.after: No carts. No smoke. No voices.

changes.before: Still not proof. One house name in Lordaeron could mean rent rolls, storehouses, tenant land, old claims hanging on from a dead grandfather's bargain.
changes.after: A lead. One house name in Lordaeron could mean rent rolls, storehouses, tenant land, old claims hanging on from a dead grandfather's bargain.

changes.before: "That isn't proof."
changes.after: "It's a lead."

changes.before: Three at once was an answer already in motion.
changes.after: Three at once meant the attack was already underway.

changes.before: Millbrook Spur sat dead quiet when they rode in.
changes.after: Millbrook Spur was dead quiet when they rode in.

changes.before: Two words. One local district marker. One word that struck somewhere older in memory.
changes.after: Two words. One local district marker. One word she remembered from somewhere older.

changes.before: Sylvanas kept her finger on the map and let another thought rise behind the first.
changes.after: Sylvanas kept her finger on the map and thought of the old grain seller next.

changes.before: Millbrook Spur had cut the choices down to one.
changes.after: Outside, in the dark beyond the waystation wall, only the south road remained.

changes.before: The books were too clean. Clean. Tallies balanced. Supplier marks she had not seen before. The attached storehouse smelled of ordinary grain. Somebody had been ahead of them. She walked it once. Missing margin notes. No erasures. No blotched ink where figures had been changed in haste. Nothing careless.
changes.after: The books were too clean—balanced tallies, unfamiliar supplier marks, an attached storehouse that smelled of ordinary grain. She walked it once and found no margin notes, no erasures, no blotched ink where figures had been changed in haste. Somebody had been ahead of them.

changes.before: Jaina did not argue.
changes.after: Jaina checked the list again and said nothing.

changes.before: Lyriel, near the door, did not move.
changes.after: Lyriel held the door with one hand near her knife.

changes.before: Jaina had been watching her without crowding the silence.
changes.after: Jaina kept both hands flat on the table and waited.

changes.before: Clean.
She stopped because there was nothing else in this room worth the next hour.
Nearly worth it now.
Millbrook Spur had cut the choices down to one.
changes.after: Outside, in the dark beyond the waystation wall, only the south road remained.

changes.before: Without naming it, they had settled into marching order.
changes.after: They rode in marching order.

changes.before: Some calculation in it too.
changes.after: He had known more than he should have.

changes.before: Jaina had been watching her without crowding the silence.
changes.after: Jaina kept both hands flat on the table and waited.

changes.before: Fast cleaners always missed a seam.
changes.after: Fast cleaners always missed one thing.

changes.before: Some calculation in it too.
